Opening multiple windows and typing commands every time is incredibly annoying and wastes time. So here's an intro to concurrently.
The Browser Render Pipeline Every Frontend Engineer Should Know

The Light and Shadow of Frontend Engineering: Think It Through Before You Switch In

We're in an age where information is everywhere and easy to grab — which means we have to spend more energy thinking, comparing, and deciding. Especially when "becoming a developer" is the trendy career switch. This post is the honest version of what frontend engineering looks like — the upside (salary ceiling, opportunities, flexibility) and the downside (information anxiety, communication load, the reality of "remote" and "freedom") — so you can make an informed decision.
Using Firefox for Frontend Dev: Features and Advantages

People keep asking what browser I use and why it isn't Chrome. So here's a quick post on Firefox's frontend dev features — better-than-Chrome Flex tooling, CSS Grid inspection, event listener inspection per element, accessibility tools like color blindness simulation, in-browser font tools, and an AJAX inspector that lets you edit and resend requests directly.
